message:
test(fcp-core): pin enforcement-mode + scope classifier serde matrix (flywheel_connectors-b6x37)

Bead asks for EnforcementGroup variant Display + serde tag. fcp-core
has no type literally named EnforcementGroup; EnforcementCheckId is
already pinned by tt7js. The unpinned enforcement-mode/scope
classifier surface covers three enums:
- BudgetEnforcement (policy.rs:93) — 2 variants Warn/Deny
- RateLimitEnforcement (ratelimit.rs:214) — 3 variants Hard/Soft/
  Advisory
- RateLimitScope (ratelimit.rs:226) — 3 variants Instance/
  Credential/Global

All snake_case via rename_all. None implements Display directly so
the bead's "Display" ask projects onto serde tag form.

Coverage:
- BudgetEnforcement per-variant JSON tag (warn/deny), JSON+CBOR
  round-trip, count=2, PascalCase + unknown rejected
- RateLimitEnforcement per-variant JSON tag (hard/soft/advisory),
  JSON+CBOR round-trip, count=3, PascalCase + cross-vocabulary
  rejected
- RateLimitScope per-variant JSON tag (instance/credential/global),
  JSON+CBOR round-trip, count=3, PascalCase + cross-vocabulary
  rejected
- CBOR encodes as Text (not integer) for all three
- Pairwise distinctness within each enum
- Cross-enum: BudgetEnforcement::Deny shares "deny" token with
  VerificationDecision/PolicyPreviewDecision/ResumeDisposition
  (intentional collision)
- BudgetEnforcement::Warn vs RateLimitEnforcement::Soft use
  distinct tokens despite similar semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
